query: Current NWS forecast for Death Valley Furnace Creek daily high temperatures July 11-23 2026, and any heat ridge/heat dome building in second half of July 2026 for Death Valley California
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**NWS Official Forecast (Furnace Creek, accessed July 13, 2026):**
- Jul 13: 115°F | Jul 14: 115°F | Jul 15: 119°F | Jul 16: 120°F | Jul 17: 116°F | Jul 18: 114°F | Jul 19: 116°F
- Overnight lows 97–100°F, sunny/dry conditions

**Extended outlook (Open-Meteo model, Jul 20–23):** Continued hot pattern — Jul 20 ~118°F, Jul 21 ~120°F, Jul 22 ~110°F, Jul 23 ~117°F, with peaks near 120°F on Jul 24. Note the global model runs a few degrees cooler than NWS's basin-specific numbers.

**Heat dome situation:** There is an active, well-documented **Western U.S. heat dome** in July 2026 — the *second* of the summer (the first was over the eastern U.S./Canada Jun 28–Jul 5). FOX Weather and AccuWeather describe a prolonged, record-threatening Western heat wave; Extreme Heat Warnings are active across the Desert Southwest (lowest basins up to ~117°F); and "double heat dome" coverage warns of dangerous conditions across the U.S. in mid/late July. For Death Valley, this ridge supports highs at or slightly above the ~116–117°F seasonal normal, consistent with the 114–120°F forecast range through the window.

The full report and structured data (key facts, sources, data points) have been submitted.

key_facts: NWS official forecast (accessed Jul 13, 2026) for Furnace Creek daily highs: Jul 13 115F, Jul 14 115F, Jul 15 119F, Jul 16 120F, Jul 17 116F, Jul 18 114F, Jul 19 116F.
NWS overnight lows for the window run 97-100F; conditions sunny/dry with only an isolated 10% precip chance overnight Jul 13.
Open-Meteo model (extends to Jul 27) shows highs: Jul 20 117.8F, Jul 21 120.0F, Jul 22 110.2F, Jul 23 117.0F, Jul 24 120.7F, Jul 25-26 ~119.4F.
Open-Meteo runs a few degrees cooler than NWS's basin-specific Furnace Creek forecast; NWS numbers should be treated as the higher/official values.
A second heat dome of summer 2026 built over the western U.S. after the first (eastern U.S./Canada) dome dissipated around Jul 5.
As of Jul 8, 2026, 44 deaths were attributed to the 2026 North American heat wave; heat advisories active in Southwest U.S. and Alaska.
FOX Weather and AccuWeather reported a scorching, record-threatening Western heat dome expected to be prolonged, ahead of the North American monsoon.
Extreme Heat Warnings issued for the Desert Southwest with lowest desert basins forecast up to ~117F.
'Double heat dome' (dual high-pressure system) forecast to move across the U.S. in mid/late July 2026 with dangerous conditions warnings.
Furnace Creek climatological early/mid-July normal highs are ~116-117F; summer highs 'often top 120F' (NPS). World record 134F set there Jul 10, 1913.
NWS/WPC top-line messaging flagged record-setting heat for the Intermountain West and Northern Plains with fire-weather concerns.
